---
title: "Overlay with integrated treatment and non-integrated controls"
output: html_notebook
---


```{r}
library(Seurat)
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(tibble)
#library(AnnotationHub)
library(future)
library(dittoSeq)

install.packages("scCustomize")

library(scCustomize)
varibow_pal <- DiscretePalette_scCustomize(num_colors = 36, palette = "varibow")

library(patchwork)

library(Seurat)
library(ggplot2)
library(cowplot)
library(patchwork)

```

```{r}
#Load the seurat object for the reference rdata

invitro.cells.combined.ref <- invitro.cells.combined.clean


# Generate the DimPlot with custom colors and labels
p1 <- DimPlot(invitro.cells.combined.ref, reduction = "umap", group.by = "new_cluster_ids", label = TRUE)

print(p1)

```

```{r}
#loading packages
plan("multicore", workers = 8) 
options(future.globals.maxSize = 64000 * 1024^2) #for 128 GB RAM
#load query rdata and SCT transform 

# Load necessary libraries
library(Seurat)
library(ggplot2)
library(patchwork)

mTSCLC_subset.query <- cells.combined.p12
  
```

```{r}

# Total number of cells in the Seurat object
total_cells <- ncol(mTSCLC_subset.query)
print(paste("Total number of cells in the dataset:", total_cells))

# Count the number of cells in each cluster for SCT_snn_res.1
cluster_counts <- mTSCLC_subset.query@meta.data %>%
  group_by(SCT_snn_res.0.6) %>%
  summarise(count = n(), .groups = 'drop')

condition_counts <- mTSCLC_subset.query@meta.data %>%
  group_by(condition) %>%
  summarise(count = n(), .groups = 'drop')

# Sum of cells across all clusters
total_cells_from_clusters <- sum(cluster_counts$count)
print(paste("Total number of cells from cluster counts:", total_cells_from_clusters))

# Sum of cells across all clusters
total_cells_from_condition <- sum(condition_counts$count)
print(paste("Total number of cells from condition counts:", total_cells_from_condition))

# Print both the cluster-wise counts and check for mismatches
print("Cluster counts:")
print(cluster_counts)

# Print both the cluster-wise counts and check for mismatches
print("Condition counts:")
print(condition_counts)

# Check if the total number of cells matches the sum of cells from clusters
if (total_cells == total_cells_from_clusters) {
  print("The total cell count matches the sum of cells from the clusters.")
} else {
  print("Warning: The total cell count does NOT match the sum of cells from the clusters.")
}

# Check if the total number of cells matches the sum of cells from clusters
if (total_cells == total_cells_from_condition) {
  print("The total cell count matches the sum of cells from the condition.")
} else {
  print("Warning: The total cell count does NOT match the sum of cells from the condition.")
}

```

```{r}
library(Seurat)
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(Seurat)
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
options(future.globals.maxSize = 70 * 1024^2 * 1024)  # Set to 30 GB

# Store original coordinates for both reference and query
ref_coordinates <- Embeddings(invitro.cells.combined.ref, "umap")
original_coordinates <- Embeddings(mTSCLC_subset.query, "umap")

# Run UMAP on reference with return.model=TRUE but keep original coordinates
invitro.cells.combined.ref <- RunUMAP(
  invitro.cells.combined.ref,
  dims = 1:20,
  reduction = "pca",
  return.model = TRUE    # This is crucial for the mapping step
)
# Restore original reference coordinates
invitro.cells.combined.ref[["umap"]]@cell.embeddings <- ref_coordinates

# Run UMAP with return.model=TRUE but keep original coordinates for query
mTSCLC_subset.query <- RunUMAP(
  mTSCLC_subset.query,
  dims = 1:20,
  reduction = "pca",
  return.model = TRUE    # This is crucial for the mapping step
)
# Replace the UMAP embeddings with the original coordinates
mTSCLC_subset.query[["umap"]]@cell.embeddings <- original_coordinates

# First, SCTransform the reference dataset
invitro.cells.combined.ref <- SCTransform(invitro.cells.combined.ref, verbose = FALSE)

# SCTransform the query dataset
mTSCLC_subset.query <- SCTransform(mTSCLC_subset.query, verbose = FALSE)


# Find anchors using SCT assay
print("Finding transfer anchors...")
cell.anchors <- FindTransferAnchors(
  reference = invitro.cells.combined.ref,
  query = mTSCLC_subset.query,
  dims = 1:20,
  reference.reduction = "pca",
  normalization.method = "SCT",
  features = VariableFeatures(invitro.cells.combined.ref),
  k.filter = 200
)

# Transfer data using reference cluster information
print("Transferring data...")
predictions <- TransferData(
  anchorset = cell.anchors, 
  refdata = invitro.cells.combined.ref$new_cluster_ids,
  dims = 1:20,
  k.weight = 50
)

# Add predictions to query object
mTSCLC_subset.query <- AddMetaData(mTSCLC_subset.query, metadata = predictions)

# Check if the prediction score column exists
if ("predicted.celltype.score" %in% colnames(mTSCLC_subset.query@meta.data)) {
  score_column <- "predicted.celltype.score"
} else if ("prediction.score.max" %in% colnames(mTSCLC_subset.query@meta.data)) {
  score_column <- "prediction.score.max"
} else {
  stop("Neither 'predicted.celltype.score' nor 'prediction.score.max' found in metadata")
}

# Set a confidence threshold
confidence_threshold <- 0.9

# Add a new column for high-confidence predictions
mTSCLC_subset.query$high_conf_celltype <- ifelse(mTSCLC_subset.query[[score_column]] > confidence_threshold,
                                          mTSCLC_subset.query$predicted.id,
                                          "Uncertain")

# Map query to reference UMAP
print("Mapping query to reference UMAP...")
mTSCLC_subset.query <- MapQuery(
  anchorset = cell.anchors, 
  reference = invitro.cells.combined.ref, 
  query = mTSCLC_subset.query,
  refdata = list(celltype = "new_cluster_ids"),
  reference.reduction = "pca", 
  reduction.model = "umap"
)

# Create plots
print("Creating plots...")
# Plot 1: Original reference UMAP structure with cell types
p4 <- DimPlot(invitro.cells.combined.ref, reduction = "umap", group.by = "new_cluster_ids",
              label = TRUE, label.size = 3, repel = TRUE) + 
  NoLegend() + 
  ggtitle("Reference Cell Types (Original Structure)")

# Plot 2: mTSCLC cells mapped onto reference UMAP structure
p5 <- DimPlot(mTSCLC_subset.query, reduction = "ref.umap",
              group.by = "predicted.id",  # Show predicted cell types
              label = TRUE, label.size = 3, repel = TRUE) + 
  NoLegend() + 
  ggtitle("mTSCLC Cells Mapped to Reference Structure")

# Plot 3: Original mTSCLC UMAP colored by predicted cell types
p6 <- DimPlot(mTSCLC_subset.query, reduction = "umap",
              group.by = "predicted.id",  # Show predicted cell types on original structure
              label = TRUE, label.size = 3, repel = TRUE) + 
  NoLegend() + 
  ggtitle("mTSCLC Original Structure (Colored by Predicted Types)")

# Display all plots
print(p4)
print(p5)
print(p6)

# Save all plots
ggsave("1_reference_original.png", p4, width = 16, height = 8, dpi = 300)
ggsave("2_mTSCLC_mapped_to_reference.png", p5, width = 16, height = 8, dpi = 300)
ggsave("3_mTSCLC_original_with_predictions.png", p6, width = 16, height = 8, dpi = 300)

# Print summary of predictions
print("Summary of predicted cell types in mTSCLC:")
print(table(mTSCLC_subset.query$predicted.id))

# Print prediction confidence summary
print("Summary of high confidence vs uncertain predictions:")
print(table(mTSCLC_subset.query$high_conf_celltype != "Uncertain"))

# Create a heatmap of prediction scores by cell type
predictions_summary <- aggregate(
  mTSCLC_subset.query[[score_column]], 
  by = list(predicted_type = mTSCLC_subset.query$predicted.id),
  FUN = function(x) c(
    mean = mean(x),
    median = median(x),
    high_conf = mean(x > confidence_threshold)
  )
)

print("Prediction confidence by cell type:")
print(predictions_summary)
```


```{r}
# Visualize the results
print("Creating plots...")
p4 <- DimPlot(invitro.cells.combined.ref, reduction = "umap", group.by = "new_cluster_ids",  
              label = TRUE, label.size = 3, repel = TRUE) + 
  NoLegend() + 
  ggtitle("In Vitro to mTSCLC Reference")

p5 <- DimPlot(mTSCLC_subset.query, reduction = "ref.umap", group.by = "high_conf_celltype", 
              label = TRUE, label.size = 3, repel = TRUE) + 
  NoLegend() + 
  ggtitle("In Vitro to mTSCLC High Confidence")

# Display the combined plot
print(p4)
print(p5)

# Save the plots
ggsave("reference_clusters_p4.png", p4, width = 16, height = 8, dpi = 300)
ggsave("query_clusters_p5.png", p5, width = 16, height = 8, dpi = 300)

# Print summary of transferred labels (high confidence)
print("Summary of transferred labels (high confidence):")
print(table(mTSCLC_subset.query$high_conf_celltype))

# Check the distribution of original conditions in the reference
print("Distribution of original conditions in reference cell types:")
print(table(invitro.cells.combined.ref$condition, invitro.cells.combined.ref$new_cluster_ids))

# Visualize the distribution of prediction scores
ggplot(mTSCLC_subset.query@meta.data, aes(x = .data[[score_column]])) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 50) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = confidence_threshold, color = "red", linetype = "dashed") +
  ggtitle("Distribution of Prediction Scores") +
  xlab("Prediction Score") +
  ylab("Count")

ggsave("prediction_score_distribution.png", width = 10, height = 6)

# Print the column names of the metadata to help debugging
print("Metadata column names:")
print(colnames(mTSCLC_subset.query@meta.data))
```


```{r}
# Visualization of prediction scores
p7 <- ggplot(mTSCLC_subset.query@meta.data, aes(x = .data[[score_column]])) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 50, fill = "skyblue", color = "black") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = confidence_threshold, color = "red", linetype = "dashed") +
  ggtitle("Distribution of Prediction Scores") +
  xlab("Prediction Score") +
  ylab("Count") +
  theme_minimal()

# Add a violin plot to show distribution by predicted cell type
p8 <- ggplot(mTSCLC_subset.query@meta.data, aes(x = predicted.id, y = .data[[score_column]])) +
  geom_violin(fill = "skyblue") +
  geom_boxplot(width = 0.1) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = confidence_threshold, color = "red", linetype = "dashed") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)) +
  ggtitle("Prediction Scores by Cell Type") +
  xlab("Predicted Cell Type") +
  ylab("Prediction Score")


# Visualization of prediction scores
p9 <-ggplot(mTSCLC_subset.query@meta.data, aes(x = .data[[score_column]])) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 50, fill = "skyblue", color = "black") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = confidence_threshold, color = "red", linetype = "dashed") +
  ggtitle("Distribution of Prediction Scores") +
  xlab("Prediction Score") +
  ylab("Count") +
  theme_minimal()

# Add a violin plot to show distribution by predicted cell type
p10 <- ggplot(mTSCLC_subset.query@meta.data, aes(x = predicted.id, y = .data[[score_column]])) +
  geom_violin(fill = "skyblue") +
  geom_boxplot(width = 0.1) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = confidence_threshold, color = "red", linetype = "dashed") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)) +
  ggtitle("Prediction Scores by Cell Type") +
  xlab("Predicted Cell Type") +
  ylab("Prediction Score")

# Display all plots
print(p7)
print(p8)
print(p9)
print(p10)

# Save all plots
ggsave("Distribution of scores.png", p7, width = 16, height = 8, dpi = 300)
ggsave("Violin of scores.png", p8, width = 16, height = 8, dpi = 300)
ggsave("Distribution of cell types.png", p9, width = 16, height = 8, dpi = 300)
ggsave("Violin of cell types.png", p10, width = 16, height = 8, dpi = 300)
```


```{r}
# Get total number of cells
total_cells <- ncol(mTSCLC_subset.query)

# All predictions (including uncertain)
print("All predictions (including low confidence):")
all_predictions <- table(mTSCLC_subset.query$predicted.id)
print(all_predictions)
print(paste("Total cells:", total_cells))
print(paste("Percentage of total cells:"))
print(round(prop.table(all_predictions) * 100, 2))

# High confidence predictions only
print("\nHigh confidence predictions only:")
high_conf_predictions <- table(mTSCLC_subset.query$predicted.id[mTSCLC_subset.query$high_conf_celltype != "Uncertain"])
print(high_conf_predictions)
print(paste("Total high confidence cells:", sum(high_conf_predictions)))
print(paste("Percentage of total cells that are high confidence:"))
print(round(prop.table(high_conf_predictions) * 100, 2))

# Create a comparison data frame
comparison_df <- data.frame(
  Cell_Type = names(all_predictions),
  Total_Predictions = as.numeric(all_predictions),
  High_Confidence = as.numeric(high_conf_predictions[names(all_predictions)]),
  row.names = NULL
)

# Calculate percentages and differences
comparison_df <- comparison_df %>%
  mutate(
    Percent_Total = round(Total_Predictions / total_cells * 100, 2),
    Percent_High_Conf = round(High_Confidence / total_cells * 100, 2),
    Percent_Confident = round(High_Confidence / Total_Predictions * 100, 2)
  )

# Print formatted comparison table
print("\nDetailed comparison:")
print(comparison_df)

# Optional: Create a visualization of the comparison
ggplot(comparison_df, aes(x = Cell_Type)) +
  geom_bar(aes(y = Total_Predictions, fill = "All Predictions"), stat = "identity", position = "dodge", alpha = 0.6) +
  geom_bar(aes(y = High_Confidence, fill = "High Confidence"), stat = "identity", position = "dodge", alpha = 0.8) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("All Predictions" = "grey", "High Confidence" = "darkblue")) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)) +
  labs(title = "Comparison of All vs High Confidence Predictions",
       x = "Cell Type",
       y = "Number of Cells",
       fill = "Prediction Type")

# Save the plot
ggsave("prediction_confidence_comparison.png", width = 10, height = 6)
```
```{r}
# Get total number of cells
total_cells <- 6104

# Get all unique cell types first to ensure we capture all categories
all_cell_types <- unique(mTSCLC_subset.query$predicted.id)

# Create the counts separately first
total_pred_table <- table(mTSCLC_subset.query$predicted.id)
high_conf_table <- table(mTSCLC_subset.query$predicted.id[mTSCLC_subset.query$high_conf_celltype != "Uncertain"])

# Create a data frame with all cell types and fill in the counts
comparison_df <- data.frame(
  Cell_Type = all_cell_types,
  Total_Predictions = as.numeric(total_pred_table[all_cell_types]),
  High_Confidence = 0  # Initialize with zeros
) 

# Fill in high confidence counts where they exist
high_conf_cell_types <- names(high_conf_table)
comparison_df$High_Confidence[match(high_conf_cell_types, comparison_df$Cell_Type)] <- 
  as.numeric(high_conf_table)

# Calculate percentages
comparison_df <- comparison_df %>%
  mutate(
    Percent_Total = round(Total_Predictions / total_cells * 100, 2),
    Percent_High_Conf = round(High_Confidence / total_cells * 100, 2),
    Percent_Confident = round(High_Confidence / Total_Predictions * 100, 2)
  )

# Create labels for the legend
total_count <- sum(comparison_df$Total_Predictions)
high_conf_count <- sum(comparison_df$High_Confidence)
total_percent <- round(total_count/total_cells * 100, 1)
high_conf_percent <- round(high_conf_count/total_cells * 100, 1)

legend_labels <- c(
  `All Predictions` = paste0("All Predictions\n(", total_count, " cells, ", total_percent, "%)"),
  `High Confidence` = paste0("High Confidence\n(", high_conf_count, " cells, ", high_conf_percent, "%)")
)

# Create the visualization
ggplot(comparison_df, aes(x = Cell_Type)) +
  geom_bar(aes(y = Total_Predictions, fill = "All Predictions"), 
           stat = "identity", 
           position = "dodge", 
           alpha = 0.6) +
  geom_bar(aes(y = High_Confidence, fill = "High Confidence"), 
           stat = "identity", 
           position = "dodge", 
           alpha = 0.8) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c("All Predictions" = "grey", "High Confidence" = "darkblue"),
    labels = legend_labels
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    limits = c(0, total_cells),
    expand = c(0, 0),
    breaks = seq(0, total_cells, by = 2000)
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = 16),  # Increased size from default
    legend.position = "top",
    legend.box.spacing = unit(0.5, "cm"),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 16),
    plot.title = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold"),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 16)
) +
  labs(
    title = "Comparison of All vs High Confidence Predictions",
    subtitle = paste0("Total cells analyzed: ", total_cells),
    x = "Cell Type",
    y = "Number of Cells",
    fill = "Prediction Type"
  )

# Save the plot
ggsave(
  "prediction_confidence_comparison.png", 
  width = 12, 
  height = 8, 
  dpi = 300
)

# Print summary statistics
print("Summary Statistics:")
print(paste("Total cells analyzed:", total_cells))
print(paste("Total high confidence cells:", high_conf_count))
print(paste("Percent high confidence:", high_conf_percent, "%"))
print("\nDetailed statistics by cell type:")
print(comparison_df)

# Save detailed statistics
write.csv(comparison_df, "cell_type_prediction_statistics.csv", row.names = FALSE)
```

```{r}
library(Seurat)
library(ggplot2)
library(patchwork)
library(ggrepel)
library(dplyr)
library(viridis)

# Define custom colors for cell types using your color scheme
cell_colors <- c(
  "mXEN" = "#D98880",
  "mTSC" = "#F39C12",
  "Serum/LIF mESC" = "#F4D03F",
  "2i/LIF mESC" = "#52BE80",
  "Amniotic Mesoderm" = "#5DADE2",
  "Amniotic Ectoderm" = "#AF7AC5",
  "Uncertain" = "#515151"  # Keep grey for uncertain cells
)

# 1. Reference structure
p1 <- DimPlot(invitro.cells.combined.ref, 
              reduction = "umap",
              group.by = "new_cluster_ids", 
              label = FALSE, 
              repel = TRUE,
              cols = cell_colors) +
  ggtitle("Reference Structure") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    axis.line = element_line(color = "black"),
    plot.title = element_text(size = 20, face = "bold"),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 18, face = "bold"),
    axis.text = element_text(size = 14),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 20),
    legend.key.size = unit(5, "lines"),
    legend.title = element_blank()
  )

# 2. Original structure with all predictions
p2_all <- DimPlot(mTSCLC_subset.query, 
                  reduction = "umap",
                  group.by = "predicted.id", 
                  label = FALSE, 
                  repel = TRUE,
                  cols = cell_colors) +
  ggtitle("All Predictions") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    axis.line = element_line(color = "black"),
    plot.title = element_text(size = 20, face = "bold"),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 18, face = "bold"),
    axis.text = element_text(size = 14),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 20),
    legend.key.size = unit(5, "lines"),
    legend.title = element_blank()
  )

# 3. High confidence predictions with layered plotting
umap_coords <- Embeddings(mTSCLC_subset.query, "umap")
plot_data <- data.frame(
  UMAP_1 = umap_coords[,1],
  UMAP_2 = umap_coords[,2],
  celltype = mTSCLC_subset.query$high_conf_celltype
)

p2_high_conf <- ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = UMAP_1, y = UMAP_2, color = celltype)) +
  # Plot uncertain cells first
  geom_point(data = subset(plot_data, celltype == "Uncertain"),
             color = "#515151", alpha = 0.2, size = 0.5) +
  # Plot high confidence cells on top
  geom_point(data = subset(plot_data, celltype != "Uncertain"),
             size = 1,
             alpha = 0.6) +
  scale_color_manual(values = cell_colors) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    axis.line = element_line(color = "black"),
    plot.title = element_text(size = 20, face = "bold"),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 18, face = "bold"),
    axis.text = element_text(size = 14),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 20),
    legend.key.size = unit(5, "lines"),
    legend.title = element_blank()
  ) + 
  guides(color = guide_legend(override.aes = list(size = 6, alpha = 1))) +
  ggtitle("High Confidence Cell Type Predictions") + 
  coord_fixed()

# Create a combined plot
combined_plot <- p1 | p2_all | p2_high_conf

# Save plots
ggsave("1_reference_structure.png", p1, width = 10, height = 8, dpi = 300)
ggsave("2_all_predictions.png", p2_all, width = 10, height = 8, dpi = 300)
ggsave("3_high_confidence_predictions.png", p2_high_conf, width = 10, height = 8, dpi = 300)
ggsave("combined_umap_visualization.png", combined_plot, width = 24, height = 8, dpi = 300)

# Print cell type statistics
print("All predictions (including uncertain):")
print(table(mTSCLC_subset.query$predicted.id))

print("\nHigh confidence predictions only:")
high_conf_counts <- table(mTSCLC_subset.query$high_conf_celltype[mTSCLC_subset.query$high_conf_celltype != "Uncertain"])
print(high_conf_counts)

# Calculate and print percentages
total_cells <- sum(table(mTSCLC_subset.query$high_conf_celltype))
percentages <- (table(mTSCLC_subset.query$high_conf_celltype) / total_cells) * 100

print("\nPercentages of each cell type:")
print(round(percentages, 2))

# Create a summary data frame
summary_df <- data.frame(
  CellType = names(high_conf_counts),
  Count = as.numeric(high_conf_counts),
  Percentage = round(as.numeric(high_conf_counts) / sum(high_conf_counts) * 100, 2)
)

# Save summary statistics
write.csv(summary_df, "cell_type_summary.csv", row.names = FALSE)
```
```{r}
cluster_color_palette <- c(
  "mXEN" = "#D98880",
  "mTSC" = "#F39C12",
  "Serum/LIF mESC" = "#F4D03F",
  "2i/LIF mESC" = "#52BE80",
  "Amniotic Mesoderm" = "#5DADE2",
  "Amniotic Ectoderm" = "#AF7AC5",
  "Uncertain" = "#515151"  # Keep grey for uncertain cells
)
# Stacked bar chart of cell type proportions
ggplot(data.frame(celltype = mTSCLC_subset.query$high_conf_celltype), aes(x = "All", fill = celltype)) +
  geom_bar(position = "fill") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = cluster_color_palette) +
  theme_minimal() +
  ggtitle("Proportion of Predicted Cell Types")

# Prediction score distributions by cell type
ggplot(mTSCLC_subset.query@meta.data, aes(x = predicted.id, y = prediction.score.max)) +
  geom_violin(aes(fill = predicted.id)) +
  geom_boxplot(width = 0.1) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = cluster_color_palette) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))
```
```{r}
library(Seurat)
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(gridExtra)
library(ggtext)

# Define extended color palette for clusters
color_palette <- c(
  "#FFD700",  # Gold (Topaz-like)
  "#FF8C00",  # Dark Orange (Citrine-like)
  "#FF4500",  # Orange Red (Fire Opal-like)
  "#DC143C",  # Crimson (Ruby-like)
  "#8B0000",  # Dark Red 
  "#800080",  # Purple (Alexandrite-like)
  "#4B0082",  # Indigo (Sapphire-like)
  "#9400D3",  # Dark Violet (Amethyst-like)
  "#6A5ACD",  # Slate Blue (Tanzanite-like)
  "#4682B4",  # Steel Blue (Aquamarine-like)
  "#00BFFF",  # Deep Sky Blue (Blue Topaz-like)
  "#008080",  # Teal (Emerald-like)
  "#20B2AA",  # Light Sea Green (Turquoise-like)
  "#32CD32",  # Lime Green (Peridot-like)
  "#00FF00"   # Bright Lime
)

# Filter for high confidence predictions
high_conf_data <- mTSCLC_subset.query@meta.data %>%
  filter(high_conf_celltype != "Uncertain") %>%
  mutate(cluster = as.character(SCT_snn_res.0.6))

# Calculate number of cells per cluster and predicted cell type
cluster_celltype_counts <- table(high_conf_data$cluster,
                               high_conf_data$high_conf_celltype)

# Calculate percentages
celltype_cluster_percentages <- prop.table(cluster_celltype_counts, margin = 2) * 100

# Convert to data frame
df <- as.data.frame(celltype_cluster_percentages)
colnames(df) <- c("Cluster", "CellType", "Percentage")

# Add count information
df$Count <- as.vector(cluster_celltype_counts)

# Calculate total cells per cell type
total_cells_per_celltype <- colSums(cluster_celltype_counts)

# Add total cells per cell type to the dataframe
df$TotalInCellType <- total_cells_per_celltype[df$CellType]

# Function to create a single pie chart
create_pie_chart <- function(cell_type) {
  celltype_data <- df[df$CellType == cell_type,]
  
  # Create custom labels with counts and percentages
  custom_labels <- paste0(
    "Cluster ", celltype_data$Cluster, 
    "\n(", celltype_data$Count, "/", celltype_data$TotalInCellType, ")",
    "\n", sprintf("%.1f%%", celltype_data$Percentage)
  )
  
  total_cells <- sum(celltype_data$Count)
  
  p <- ggplot(celltype_data, aes(x = "", y = Percentage, fill = Cluster)) +
    geom_bar(stat = "identity", width = 1) +
    coord_polar("y", start = 0) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = color_palette[1:length(unique(df$Cluster))],
                     labels = custom_labels) +
    theme_void() +
    theme(
      legend.position = "right",
      legend.title = element_text(size = 16),
      legend.text = element_text(size = 12),
      legend.key.size = unit(2, "cm"),
      plot.title = element_text(size = 20, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
      plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 16, hjust = 0.5)
    ) +
    ggtitle(cell_type) +
    labs(subtitle = paste("Total high confidence cells:", total_cells)) +
    guides(fill = guide_legend(title = "Cluster\n(Count/Total)\nPercentage",
                             override.aes = list(size = 1)))
  
  return(p)
}

# Create pie charts for each cell type
pie_charts <- lapply(sort(unique(df$CellType)), create_pie_chart)

# Arrange pie charts in a grid
grid_arranged <- grid.arrange(grobs = pie_charts, ncol = 3)

# Save the grid of pie charts
ggsave("high_confidence_celltype_by_cluster_pie_charts.png",
       grid_arranged,
       width = 30,
       height = 20,
       units = "in",
       dpi = 300)

# Save data to CSV
write.csv(df, "high_confidence_celltype_by_cluster_percentages_and_counts.csv",
          row.names = FALSE)

# Print summaries
print("Summary of high confidence predictions by cluster:")
print(table(high_conf_data$cluster, high_conf_data$high_conf_celltype))

print("\nCluster contribution to each cell type (%):")
print(celltype_cluster_percentages)

print("\nTotal cells per cell type:")
print(total_cells_per_celltype)

# Additional analysis: Cluster composition
print("\nPercentage of each cluster that is high confidence:")
cluster_totals <- table(mTSCLC_subset.query$SCT_snn_res.0.6)
high_conf_by_cluster <- table(high_conf_data$cluster)
cluster_percentages <- (high_conf_by_cluster / cluster_totals) * 100
print(cluster_percentages)
```

```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(pheatmap)
library(RColorBrewer)

cluster_color_palette <- c(
  "#FFD700",  # Gold (Topaz-like)
  "#FF8C00",  # Dark Orange (Citrine-like)
  "#FF4500",  # Orange Red (Fire Opal-like)
  "#DC143C",  # Crimson (Ruby-like)
  "#8B0000",  # Dark Red 
  "#800080",  # Purple (Alexandrite-like)
  "#4B0082",  # Indigo (Sapphire-like)
  "#9400D3",  # Dark Violet (Amethyst-like)
  "#6A5ACD",  # Slate Blue (Tanzanite-like)
  "#4682B4",  # Steel Blue (Aquamarine-like)
  "#00BFFF",  # Deep Sky Blue (Blue Topaz-like)
  "#008080",  # Teal (Emerald-like)
  "#20B2AA",  # Light Sea Green (Turquoise-like)
  "#32CD32",  # Lime Green (Peridot-like)
  "#00FF00"   # Bright Lime
)

# Reshape the data for the heatmap
heatmap_data <- final_counts %>%
  select(Cluster, predicted.celltype, percentage_after) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = predicted.celltype, values_from = percentage_after, values_fill = list(percentage_after = 0))

# Add Amniotic Mesoderm column with 0%
heatmap_data$`Amniotic Mesoderm` <- 0

# Ensure all cell types of interest are present
for (cell_type in cell_types_of_interest) {
  if (!(cell_type %in% colnames(heatmap_data))) {
    heatmap_data[[cell_type]] <- 0
  }
}

# Convert to matrix while preserving row names
heatmap_matrix <- as.matrix(heatmap_data %>% column_to_rownames("Cluster"))

# Reorder columns to match cell_types_of_interest
heatmap_matrix <- heatmap_matrix[, cell_types_of_interest]

print("Heatmap Matrix dimensions:")
print(dim(heatmap_matrix))
print("Heatmap Matrix row names:")
print(rownames(heatmap_matrix))
print("Heatmap Matrix column names:")
print(colnames(heatmap_matrix))

# Create annotations
col_annotation <- data.frame(CellType = factor(colnames(heatmap_matrix), levels = cell_types_of_interest))
rownames(col_annotation) <- colnames(heatmap_matrix)

row_annotation <- data.frame(Cluster = factor(rownames(heatmap_matrix)))
rownames(row_annotation) <- rownames(heatmap_matrix)

print("Column Annotation:")
print(col_annotation)
print("Row Annotation:")
print(row_annotation)

# Define color palettes
celltype_colors <- setNames(
  colorRampPalette(cell_type_colors)(length(cell_types_of_interest)),
  cell_types_of_interest
)

print("Cell Type Colors:")
print(celltype_colors)

cluster_colors <- setNames(
  cluster_color_palette[1:nrow(heatmap_matrix)],
  rownames(heatmap_matrix)
)

print("Cluster Colors:")
print(cluster_colors)

# Define annotation colors
annotation_colors <- list(
  CellType = celltype_colors,
  Cluster = cluster_colors
)

print("Annotation Colors:")
print(str(annotation_colors))

# Create the heatmap
png("mTSCLC_celltype_percentage_heatmap_fixed.png", width = 6000, height = 5000, res = 300)
pheatmap(heatmap_matrix,
         display_numbers = TRUE,
         number_format = "%.2f%%",
         number_color = "white",
         cluster_rows = TRUE,
         cluster_cols = FALSE,
         color = colorRampPalette(c("#000080", "#4682B4", "#800020"))(100),
         main = "In Vitro Cell Types Identified in mTSCLC clusters",
         fontsize = 20,
         fontsize_number = 20,
         cellwidth = 100,
         cellheight = 60,
         angle_col="45",
         annotation_col = col_annotation,
         annotation_row = row_annotation,
         annotation_colors = annotation_colors,
                  annotation_names_row = FALSE,
         annotation_names_col = FALSE,
         border_color = "darkgrey",
          show_rownames = TRUE,
                  treeheight_row = 400,
         treeheight_col = 0,
         fontsize_row = 18,
         fontsize_col = 18)
dev.off()

print("Heatmap creation attempt completed.")
```
```{r}
# Density plots of prediction scores
ggplot(mTSCLC_subset.query@meta.data, aes(x = prediction.score.max)) +
  geom_density(aes(fill = predicted.id), alpha = 0.5) +
  facet_wrap(~predicted.id) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = cluster_color_palette)

# Spatial distribution of prediction confidence
FeaturePlot(mTSCLC_subset.query, 
           features = "prediction.score.max",
           reduction = "umap")
```
```{r}
library(pheatmap)
# Set identity for reference dataset
Idents(invitro.cells.combined.ref) <- "new_cluster_ids"

# Define cell types
cell_types <- c("Serum/LIF mESC", "2i/LIF mESC", "mTSC", "mXEN")

# Create empty lists to store results
all_markers_list <- list()
top_genes_list <- list()

# Function to get expression info for a gene list
get_expression_info <- function(object, genes, cell_type) {
  # Calculate percentage expressed
  pct_expressed <- rowMeans(GetAssayData(object, slot = "counts")[genes, 
                           WhichCells(object, idents = cell_type)] > 0) * 100
  
  # Calculate average expression
  avg_exp <- rowMeans(GetAssayData(object, slot = "data")[genes, 
                      WhichCells(object, idents = cell_type)])
  
  return(data.frame(
    gene = genes,
    pct_expressed = pct_expressed,
    avg_expression = avg_exp
  ))
}

# Find markers for each cell type compared to all others
for(cell_type in cell_types) {
  markers <- FindMarkers(invitro.cells.combined.ref,
                        ident.1 = cell_type,
                        ident.2 = setdiff(cell_types, cell_type),
                        min.pct = 0.25)
  
  # Store full results
  all_markers_list[[cell_type]] <- markers
  
  # Get top 100 upregulated genes with statistics
  top_genes_df <- markers %>%
    filter(avg_log2FC > 1) %>%
    arrange(desc(avg_log2FC)) %>%
    head(100)
  
  # Get expression info for top genes
  exp_info <- get_expression_info(invitro.cells.combined.ref, 
                                rownames(top_genes_df), 
                                cell_type)
  
  # Combine marker statistics with expression info
  final_df <- data.frame(
    gene = rownames(top_genes_df),
    avg_log2FC = top_genes_df$avg_log2FC,
    p_val_adj = top_genes_df$p_val_adj,
    pct_in_cell_type = top_genes_df$pct.1 * 100,
    pct_in_others = top_genes_df$pct.2 * 100
  ) %>%
    left_join(exp_info, by = "gene")
  
  # Store gene list
  top_genes_list[[cell_type]] <- final_df$gene
  
  # Print top 10 markers with expression info
  cat(sprintf("\nTop 10 %s markers:\n", cell_type))
  print(head(final_df, 10))
  
  # Save detailed gene lists
  write.csv(final_df,
            file = sprintf("top_100_%s_genes_with_expression.csv", 
                         gsub("[/ ]", "_", tolower(cell_type))),
            row.names = FALSE)
}

# Create a combined table with expression info for all cell types
combined_expression_table <- data.frame()

for(cell_type in cell_types) {
  genes <- top_genes_list[[cell_type]]
  exp_info <- get_expression_info(invitro.cells.combined.ref, genes, cell_type)
  
  # Add cell type specific columns
  colnames(exp_info) <- c("gene",
                         paste0(gsub("[/ ]", "_", tolower(cell_type)), "_pct_expressed"),
                         paste0(gsub("[/ ]", "_", tolower(cell_type)), "_avg_expression"))
  
  if(nrow(combined_expression_table) == 0) {
    combined_expression_table <- exp_info
  } else {
    combined_expression_table <- full_join(combined_expression_table, exp_info, by = "gene")
  }
}

# Save combined expression table
write.csv(combined_expression_table,
          file = "all_marker_genes_expression.csv",
          row.names = FALSE)

# Calculate scores for D0D4 object
# First ensure the genes exist in the dataset
genes_present <- lapply(top_genes_list, function(genes) {
  intersect(genes, rownames(cells.combined.p12))
})

# Print number of genes found for each cell type
for(cell_type in names(genes_present)) {
  cat(sprintf("Number of %s genes found in dataset: %d\n",
              cell_type, length(genes_present[[cell_type]])))
}

# Calculate scores for each cell type
for(cell_type in names(genes_present)) {
  score_name <- gsub("[/ ]", "_", tolower(cell_type))
 cells.combined.p12 <- AddModuleScore(
    object = cells.combined.p12,
    features = list(genes_present[[cell_type]]),
    name = paste0(score_name, "_score")
  )
}

# Create visualization with controlled scales
# Get all score columns
score_columns <- grep("_score1$", colnames(cells.combined.p12@meta.data), value = TRUE)

# Find maximum absolute value across all scores for symmetric scaling
max_abs_value <- max(abs(as.matrix(cells.combined.p12@meta.data[, score_columns])))

# Create feature plots
plot_list <- list()
for(score in score_columns) {
  plot_title <- gsub("_score1$", "", score)
  plot_title <- gsub("_", "/", toupper(plot_title))
  
  p <- FeaturePlot(cells.combined.p12,
                   features = score,
                   reduction = "umap",
                   order = TRUE) +
    scale_color_gradientn(
      colors = c("navy", "lightgrey", "red"),
      limits = c(-max_abs_value, max_abs_value)
    ) +
    theme_minimal() +
    ggtitle(plot_title) +
    theme(
      plot.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold"),
      axis.title = element_text(size = 12),
      legend.title = element_text(size = 10),
      legend.text = element_text(size = 8)
    ) + 
    coord_fixed()
  
  plot_list[[score]] <- p
}

# Combine plots using patchwork
score_plots <- wrap_plots(plot_list, ncol = 2)

# Save the plots
ggsave("cell_type_scores.png", score_plots, width = 15, height = 14, dpi = 300)

# Create categorical plots based on scores
score_cutoff <- 0  # Adjust based on score distribution
for(score in score_columns) {
  category_name <- gsub("_score1$", "_category", score)
  cell_type_name <- gsub("_score1$", "", score)
  cells.combined.p12@meta.data[[category_name]] <- ifelse(
    cells.combined.p12@meta.data[[score]] > score_cutoff,
    paste("High", toupper(cell_type_name)),
    paste("Low", toupper(cell_type_name))
  )
}

# Create categorical UMAPs
category_columns <- grep("_category$", colnames(cells.combined.p12@meta.data), value = TRUE)
categorical_umaps <- DimPlot(cells.combined.p12,
                           reduction = "umap",
                           group.by = category_columns,
                           ncol = 2,
                           cols = c(
                             "High SERUM_LIF_MESC" = "#F4D03F",
                             "High 2I_LIF_MESC" = "#27AE60",
                             "High MTSC" = "#F39C12",
                             "High MXEN" = "#8E44AD",
                             "Low SERUM_LIF_MESC" = "grey80",
                             "Low 2I_LIF_MESC" = "grey80",
                             "Low MTSC" = "grey80",
                             "Low MXEN" = "grey80"
                           )) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold"),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 12),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 10),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 8)
  )

ggsave("categorical_scores.png", categorical_umaps, width = 15, height = 14, dpi = 300)

# Print score statistics
cat("\nScore statistics:\n")
for(score in score_columns) {
  cat(sprintf("\n%s scores:\n", gsub("_score1$", "", toupper(score))))
  print(summary(cells.combined.p12@meta.data[[score]]))
}

# Calculate correlation matrix between scores
score_matrix <- as.matrix(cells.combined.p12@meta.data[, score_columns])
correlation_matrix <- cor(score_matrix)
cat("\nCorrelation matrix between scores:\n")
print(correlation_matrix)

# Optional: Create correlation heatmap
correlation_plot <- pheatmap(
  correlation_matrix,
  display_numbers = TRUE,
  main = "Score Correlations",
  fontsize = 10,
  fontsize_number = 8
)

# Save correlation heatmap
pdf("score_correlations.pdf", width = 8, height = 8)
print(correlation_plot)
dev.off()
```

```{r}
# Find DEGs between Serum/LIF mESC and mTSC from reference dataset
Idents(invitro.cells.combined.ref) <- "new_cluster_ids"

# Get Serum/LIF mESC markers (comparing to mTSC)
mesc_markers <- FindMarkers(invitro.cells.combined.ref,
                          ident.1 = "Serum/LIF mESC",
                          ident.2 = "mTSC",
                          min.pct = 0.25)

# Get mTSC markers (comparing to Serum/LIF mESC)
mtsc_markers <- FindMarkers(invitro.cells.combined.ref,
                         ident.1 = "mTSC",
                         ident.2 = "Serum/LIF mESC",
                         min.pct = 0.25)

# Get top 100 genes for each cell type
mesc_genes <- rownames(mesc_markers %>%
                     filter(avg_log2FC > 1) %>%
                     arrange(desc(avg_log2FC)) %>%
                     head(100))

mtsc_genes <- rownames(mtsc_markers %>%
                    filter(avg_log2FC > 1) %>%
                    arrange(desc(avg_log2FC)) %>%
                    head(100))

# Print top genes for verification
print("Top 10 Serum/LIF mESC markers:")
print(head(mesc_genes, 10))
print("\nTop 10 mTSC markers:")
print(head(mtsc_genes, 10))

# Save gene lists
write.csv(data.frame(gene = mesc_genes), "top_100_mesc_genes.csv")
write.csv(data.frame(gene = mtsc_genes), "top_100_mtsc_genes.csv")

# Calculate scores for mTSCLC object
# First ensure the genes exist in the dataset
mesc_genes_present <- intersect(mesc_genes, rownames(cells.combined.p12))
mtsc_genes_present <- intersect(mtsc_genes, rownames(cells.combined.p12))

print(paste("Number of Serum/LIF mESC genes found in dataset:", length(mesc_genes_present)))
print(paste("Number of mTSC genes found in dataset:", length(mtsc_genes_present)))

# Calculate scores
cells.combined.p12 <- AddModuleScore(
    object = cells.combined.p12,
    features = list(mesc_genes_present),
    name = "mESC_score"
)

cells.combined.p12 <- AddModuleScore(
    object = cells.combined.p12,
    features = list(mtsc_genes_present),
    name = "mTSC_score"
)

# Create visualization with controlled scales
# First get the maximum absolute value for symmetric scaling
max_abs_value <- max(abs(c(
    cells.combined.p12$mESC_score1,
    cells.combined.p12$mTSC_score1)))

# Create plots with symmetric scales and custom colors
p1 <- FeaturePlot(cells.combined.p12,
                  features = "mESC_score1",
                  reduction = "umap",
                  order = TRUE) +
    scale_color_gradientn(
        colors = c("navy", "lightgrey", "red"),
        limits = c(-max_abs_value, max_abs_value)
    ) +
    theme_minimal() +
    ggtitle("Serum/LIF mESC Score") +
    theme(
        plot.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold"),
        axis.title = element_text(size = 12),
        legend.title = element_text(size = 10),
        legend.text = element_text(size = 8)
    )

p2 <- FeaturePlot(cells.combined.p12,
                  features = "mTSC_score1",
                  reduction = "umap",
                  order = TRUE) +
    scale_color_gradientn(
        colors = c("navy", "lightgrey", "red"),
        limits = c(-max_abs_value, max_abs_value)
    ) +
    theme_minimal() +
    ggtitle("mTSC Score") +
    theme(
        plot.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold"),
        axis.title = element_text(size = 12),
        legend.title = element_text(size = 10),
        legend.text = element_text(size = 8)
    )

# Combine plots
score_plots <- p1 | p2

# Save the plots
ggsave("cell_type_scores.png", score_plots, width = 15, height = 7, dpi = 300)

# Create categorical plots based on scores
score_cutoff <- 0  # You might want to adjust this based on the score distribution
cells.combined.p12$mESC_category <- ifelse(cells.combined.p12$mESC_score1 > score_cutoff, "High mESC", "Low mESC")
cells.combined.p12$mTSC_category <- ifelse(cells.combined.p12$mTSC_score1 > score_cutoff, "High mTSC", "Low mTSC")

# Create categorical UMAPs with custom colors
categorical_umaps <- DimPlot(cells.combined.p12,
                           reduction = "umap",
                           group.by = c("mESC_category", "mTSC_category"),
                           ncol = 2,
                           cols = c(
                               "High mESC" = "#F4D03F",
                               "Low mESC" = "grey80",
                               "High mTSC" = "#F39C12",
                               "Low mTSC" = "grey80"
                           )) +
    theme(
        plot.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold"),
        axis.title = element_text(size = 12),
        legend.title = element_text(size = 10),
        legend.text = element_text(size = 8)
    )

ggsave("categorical_scores.png", categorical_umaps, width = 15, height = 7, dpi = 300)

# Print score statistics
print("\nScore statistics:")
print("Serum/LIF mESC scores:")
print(summary(cells.combined.p12$mESC_score1))
print("\nmTSC scores:")
print(summary(cells.combined.p12$mTSC_score1))

# Calculate correlation between scores
score_correlation <- cor(cells.combined.p12$mESC_score1,cells.combined.p12$mTSC_score1)
print("\nCorrelation between mESC and mTSC scores:")
print(score_correlation)
```
```{r}
# Create a modified function to calculate enrichment
calculate_cluster_enrichment <- function(seurat_obj, gene_list, cluster_idents) {
  # Initialize results dataframe
  results <- data.frame()
  
  # Get expression matrix
  expr_matrix <- GetAssayData(seurat_obj, slot = "data")
  
  # For each cluster
  for(cluster in unique(cluster_idents)) {
    # Get mean expression in cluster vs out of cluster
    cluster_cells <- cluster_idents == cluster
    
    # Calculate mean expression for each gene in and out of cluster
    cluster_means <- rowMeans(expr_matrix[gene_list, cluster_cells, drop = FALSE])
    other_means <- rowMeans(expr_matrix[gene_list, !cluster_cells, drop = FALSE])
    
    # Perform Wilcoxon test on the gene means
    test_result <- wilcox.test(cluster_means, other_means)
    
    # Add to results
    results <- rbind(results, data.frame(
      Cluster = cluster,
      Mean_Expression_In_Cluster = mean(cluster_means),
      Mean_Expression_Other = mean(other_means),
      Fold_Change = mean(cluster_means) / mean(other_means),
      P_Value = test_result$p.value
    ))
  }
  
  # Adjust p-values
  results$FDR <- p.adjust(results$P_Value, method = "BH")
  
  # Sort by fold change
  results <- results[order(-results$Fold_Change),]
  
  return(results)
}

# Calculate enrichment
enrichment_results <- calculate_cluster_enrichment(
  cells.combined.p12, 
  mtsc_genes_present, 
  cluster_ids
)

# Print results
print("Cluster enrichment for mTSC genes:")
print(enrichment_results)

# Create visualization
p <- ggplot(enrichment_results, 
       aes(x = reorder(Cluster, Fold_Change), y = Fold_Change)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", aes(fill = -log10(FDR))) +
  coord_flip() +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(x = "Cluster", 
       y = "Fold Change",
       title = "mTSC Gene Enrichment by Cluster",
       fill = "-log10(FDR)") +
  theme(axis.text.y = element_text(size = 10))

# Save the plot
ggsave("mtsc_gene_enrichment.png", p, width = 10, height = 8)

# Print significant results
print("\nSignificantly enriched clusters (FDR < 0.05):")
print(enrichment_results[enrichment_results$FDR < 0.05,])
```
```{r}
# Calculate enrichment with the previous function
enrichment_results <- calculate_cluster_enrichment(
  cells.combined.p12, 
  mtsc_genes_present, 
  cluster_ids
)

# Add more descriptive categories based on fold change
enrichment_results$Enrichment_Level <- case_when(
  enrichment_results$Fold_Change >= 2.0 ~ "Highly Enriched",
  enrichment_results$Fold_Change >= 1.5 ~ "Moderately Enriched",
  enrichment_results$Fold_Change >= 1.0 ~ "Slightly Enriched",
  TRUE ~ "Not Enriched"
)

# Format the numeric columns
enrichment_results$Fold_Change <- round(enrichment_results$Fold_Change, 3)
enrichment_results$Mean_Expression_In_Cluster <- round(enrichment_results$Mean_Expression_In_Cluster, 3)
enrichment_results$Mean_Expression_Other <- round(enrichment_results$Mean_Expression_Other, 3)
enrichment_results$FDR <- format(enrichment_results$FDR, scientific = TRUE, digits = 3)

# Add cluster size information
cluster_sizes <- table(cluster_ids)
enrichment_results$Cluster_Size <- cluster_sizes[match(enrichment_results$Cluster, names(cluster_sizes))]
enrichment_results$Cluster_Percentage <- round(enrichment_results$Cluster_Size / sum(cluster_sizes) * 100, 2)

# Make sure dplyr is loaded
library(dplyr)

# Reorder columns for clarity
enrichment_results <- enrichment_results %>%
  dplyr::select(
    Cluster,
    Enrichment_Level,
    Fold_Change,
    FDR,
    Cluster_Size,
    Cluster_Percentage,
    Mean_Expression_In_Cluster,
    Mean_Expression_Other
  )

# Save detailed results
write.csv(enrichment_results, "mtsc_enrichment_detailed_results.csv", row.names = FALSE)

# Print summary
print("Summary of mTSC gene enrichment analysis:")
print(enrichment_results)
```


```{r}
# Ensure the query object has expression data available
if (ncol(mTSCLC_subset.query) == 0) {
  stop("No expression data available in mTSCLC_subset.query.")
}

# Check for the presence of gene names in the query data
gene_of_interest <- "Nanog"  # Replace with the gene you're interested in

# Check if the gene of interest is present in the query dataset
if (!gene_of_interest %in% rownames(mTSCLC_subset.query)) {
  stop(paste(gene_of_interest, "is not found in the mTSCLC_subset.query dataset."))
}

# Filter the high-confidence cells
mapped_cells <- mTSCLC_subset.query[, mTSCLC_subset.query$high_conf_celltype != "Uncertain"]

# Create a feature plot for the gene of interest
feature_plot <- FeaturePlot(mapped_cells, features = gene_of_interest, 
                             reduction = "ref.umap") + 
  ggtitle(paste("Feature Plot for", gene_of_interest, "in Mapped Cells")) +
  theme_minimal()

# Save the plot
ggsave(paste0("Feature_Plot_", gene_of_interest, "_Mapped_Cells.png"), 
       feature_plot, width = 12, height = 10, dpi = 300)

# Optionally, display the plot in R
print(feature_plot)

```







```{r}
#
#
#
#
#
#
#
#

#
#
#
#
#
#
#
```

```{r}
#Load the seurat object for the reference rdata

invitro.cells.combined.ref <- invitro.cells.combined.clean


# Generate the DimPlot with custom colors and labels
p1 <- DimPlot(invitro.cells.combined.ref, reduction = "umap", group.by = "new_cluster_ids", label = TRUE)

print(p1)

```

```{r}
#loading packages
plan("multicore", workers = 8) 
options(future.globals.maxSize = 64000 * 1024^2) #for 128 GB RAM
#load query rdata and SCT transform 

# Load necessary libraries
library(Seurat)
library(ggplot2)
library(patchwork)

mTSCLC_subset.query <- subset_p12
  
```

```{r}

# Total number of cells in the Seurat object
total_cells <- ncol(mTSCLC_subset.query)
print(paste("Total number of cells in the dataset:", total_cells))

# Count the number of cells in each cluster for SCT_snn_res.1
cluster_counts <- mTSCLC_subset.query@meta.data %>%
  group_by(SCT_snn_res.0.6) %>%
  summarise(count = n(), .groups = 'drop')

condition_counts <- mTSCLC_subset.query@meta.data %>%
  group_by(condition) %>%
  summarise(count = n(), .groups = 'drop')

# Sum of cells across all clusters
total_cells_from_clusters <- sum(cluster_counts$count)
print(paste("Total number of cells from cluster counts:", total_cells_from_clusters))

# Sum of cells across all clusters
total_cells_from_condition <- sum(condition_counts$count)
print(paste("Total number of cells from condition counts:", total_cells_from_condition))

# Print both the cluster-wise counts and check for mismatches
print("Cluster counts:")
print(cluster_counts)

# Print both the cluster-wise counts and check for mismatches
print("Condition counts:")
print(condition_counts)

# Check if the total number of cells matches the sum of cells from clusters
if (total_cells == total_cells_from_clusters) {
  print("The total cell count matches the sum of cells from the clusters.")
} else {
  print("Warning: The total cell count does NOT match the sum of cells from the clusters.")
}

# Check if the total number of cells matches the sum of cells from clusters
if (total_cells == total_cells_from_condition) {
  print("The total cell count matches the sum of cells from the condition.")
} else {
  print("Warning: The total cell count does NOT match the sum of cells from the condition.")
}

```

```{r}
library(Seurat)
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(Seurat)
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
options(future.globals.maxSize = 70 * 1024^2 * 1024)  # Set to 30 GB

# Store original coordinates for both reference and query
ref_coordinates <- Embeddings(invitro.cells.combined.ref, "umap")
original_coordinates <- Embeddings(mTSCLC_subset.query, "umap")

# Run UMAP on reference with return.model=TRUE but keep original coordinates
invitro.cells.combined.ref <- RunUMAP(
  invitro.cells.combined.ref,
  dims = 1:20,
  reduction = "pca",
  return.model = TRUE    # This is crucial for the mapping step
)
# Restore original reference coordinates
invitro.cells.combined.ref[["umap"]]@cell.embeddings <- ref_coordinates

# Run UMAP with return.model=TRUE but keep original coordinates for query
mTSCLC_subset.query <- RunUMAP(
  mTSCLC_subset.query,
  dims = 1:20,
  reduction = "pca",
  return.model = TRUE    # This is crucial for the mapping step
)
# Replace the UMAP embeddings with the original coordinates
mTSCLC_subset.query[["umap"]]@cell.embeddings <- original_coordinates

# First, SCTransform the reference dataset
invitro.cells.combined.ref <- SCTransform(invitro.cells.combined.ref, verbose = FALSE)

# SCTransform the query dataset
mTSCLC_subset.query <- SCTransform(mTSCLC_subset.query, verbose = FALSE)


# Find anchors using SCT assay
print("Finding transfer anchors...")
cell.anchors <- FindTransferAnchors(
  reference = invitro.cells.combined.ref,
  query = mTSCLC_subset.query,
  dims = 1:20,
  reference.reduction = "pca",
  normalization.method = "SCT",
  features = VariableFeatures(invitro.cells.combined.ref),
  k.filter = 200
)

# Transfer data using reference cluster information
print("Transferring data...")
predictions <- TransferData(
  anchorset = cell.anchors, 
  refdata = invitro.cells.combined.ref$new_cluster_ids,
  dims = 1:20,
  k.weight = 50
)

# Add predictions to query object
mTSCLC_subset.query <- AddMetaData(mTSCLC_subset.query, metadata = predictions)

# Check if the prediction score column exists
if ("predicted.celltype.score" %in% colnames(mTSCLC_subset.query@meta.data)) {
  score_column <- "predicted.celltype.score"
} else if ("prediction.score.max" %in% colnames(mTSCLC_subset.query@meta.data)) {
  score_column <- "prediction.score.max"
} else {
  stop("Neither 'predicted.celltype.score' nor 'prediction.score.max' found in metadata")
}

# Set a confidence threshold
confidence_threshold <- 0.9

# Add a new column for high-confidence predictions
mTSCLC_subset.query$high_conf_celltype <- ifelse(mTSCLC_subset.query[[score_column]] > confidence_threshold,
                                          mTSCLC_subset.query$predicted.id,
                                          "Uncertain")

# Map query to reference UMAP
print("Mapping query to reference UMAP...")
mTSCLC_subset.query <- MapQuery(
  anchorset = cell.anchors, 
  reference = invitro.cells.combined.ref, 
  query = mTSCLC_subset.query,
  refdata = list(celltype = "new_cluster_ids"),
  reference.reduction = "pca", 
  reduction.model = "umap"
)

# Create plots
print("Creating plots...")
# Plot 1: Original reference UMAP structure with cell types
p4 <- DimPlot(invitro.cells.combined.ref, reduction = "umap", group.by = "new_cluster_ids",
              label = TRUE, label.size = 3, repel = TRUE) + 
  NoLegend() + 
  ggtitle("Reference Cell Types (Original Structure)")

# Plot 2: mTSCLC cells mapped onto reference UMAP structure
p5 <- DimPlot(mTSCLC_subset.query, reduction = "ref.umap",
              group.by = "predicted.id",  # Show predicted cell types
              label = TRUE, label.size = 3, repel = TRUE) + 
  NoLegend() + 
  ggtitle("mTSCLC Cells Mapped to Reference Structure")

# Plot 3: Original mTSCLC UMAP colored by predicted cell types
p6 <- DimPlot(mTSCLC_subset.query, reduction = "umap",
              group.by = "predicted.id",  # Show predicted cell types on original structure
              label = TRUE, label.size = 3, repel = TRUE) + 
  NoLegend() + 
  ggtitle("mTSCLC Original Structure (Colored by Predicted Types)")

# Display all plots
print(p4)
print(p5)
print(p6)

# Save all plots
ggsave("1_reference_original_subset.png", p4, width = 16, height = 8, dpi = 300)
ggsave("2_mTSCLC_mapped_to_reference_subset.png", p5, width = 16, height = 8, dpi = 300)
ggsave("3_mTSCLC_original_with_predictions_subset.png", p6, width = 16, height = 8, dpi = 300)

# Print summary of predictions
print("Summary of predicted cell types in mTSCLC:")
print(table(mTSCLC_subset.query$predicted.id))

# Print prediction confidence summary
print("Summary of high confidence vs uncertain predictions:")
print(table(mTSCLC_subset.query$high_conf_celltype != "Uncertain"))

# Create a heatmap of prediction scores by cell type
predictions_summary <- aggregate(
  mTSCLC_subset.query[[score_column]], 
  by = list(predicted_type = mTSCLC_subset.query$predicted.id),
  FUN = function(x) c(
    mean = mean(x),
    median = median(x),
    high_conf = mean(x > confidence_threshold)
  )
)

print("Prediction confidence by cell type:")
print(predictions_summary)
```


```{r}
# Visualize the results
print("Creating plots...")
p4 <- DimPlot(invitro.cells.combined.ref, reduction = "umap", group.by = "new_cluster_ids",  
              label = TRUE, label.size = 3, repel = TRUE) + 
  NoLegend() + 
  ggtitle("In Vitro to mTSCLC Reference")

p5 <- DimPlot(mTSCLC_subset.query, reduction = "ref.umap", group.by = "high_conf_celltype", 
              label = TRUE, label.size = 3, repel = TRUE) + 
  NoLegend() + 
  ggtitle("In Vitro to mTSCLC High Confidence")

# Display the combined plot
print(p4)
print(p5)

# Save the plots
ggsave("reference_clusters_p4_subset.png", p4, width = 16, height = 8, dpi = 300)
ggsave("query_clusters_p5_subset.png", p5, width = 16, height = 8, dpi = 300)

# Print summary of transferred labels (high confidence)
print("Summary of transferred labels (high confidence):")
print(table(mTSCLC_subset.query$high_conf_celltype))

# Check the distribution of original conditions in the reference
print("Distribution of original conditions in reference cell types:")
print(table(invitro.cells.combined.ref$condition, invitro.cells.combined.ref$new_cluster_ids))

# Visualize the distribution of prediction scores
ggplot(mTSCLC_subset.query@meta.data, aes(x = .data[[score_column]])) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 50) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = confidence_threshold, color = "red", linetype = "dashed") +
  ggtitle("Distribution of Prediction Scores") +
  xlab("Prediction Score") +
  ylab("Count")

ggsave("prediction_score_distribution.png", width = 10, height = 6)

# Print the column names of the metadata to help debugging
print("Metadata column names:")
print(colnames(mTSCLC_subset.query@meta.data))
```


```{r}
# Visualization of prediction scores
p7 <- ggplot(mTSCLC_subset.query@meta.data, aes(x = .data[[score_column]])) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 50, fill = "skyblue", color = "black") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = confidence_threshold, color = "red", linetype = "dashed") +
  ggtitle("Distribution of Prediction Scores") +
  xlab("Prediction Score") +
  ylab("Count") +
  theme_minimal()

# Add a violin plot to show distribution by predicted cell type
p8 <- ggplot(mTSCLC_subset.query@meta.data, aes(x = predicted.id, y = .data[[score_column]])) +
  geom_violin(fill = "skyblue") +
  geom_boxplot(width = 0.1) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = confidence_threshold, color = "red", linetype = "dashed") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)) +
  ggtitle("Prediction Scores by Cell Type") +
  xlab("Predicted Cell Type") +
  ylab("Prediction Score")


# Visualization of prediction scores
p9 <-ggplot(mTSCLC_subset.query@meta.data, aes(x = .data[[score_column]])) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 50, fill = "skyblue", color = "black") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = confidence_threshold, color = "red", linetype = "dashed") +
  ggtitle("Distribution of Prediction Scores") +
  xlab("Prediction Score") +
  ylab("Count") +
  theme_minimal()

# Add a violin plot to show distribution by predicted cell type
p10 <- ggplot(mTSCLC_subset.query@meta.data, aes(x = predicted.id, y = .data[[score_column]])) +
  geom_violin(fill = "skyblue") +
  geom_boxplot(width = 0.1) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = confidence_threshold, color = "red", linetype = "dashed") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)) +
  ggtitle("Prediction Scores by Cell Type") +
  xlab("Predicted Cell Type") +
  ylab("Prediction Score")

# Display all plots
print(p7)
print(p8)
print(p9)
print(p10)

# Save all plots
ggsave("Distribution of scores_subset.png", p7, width = 16, height = 8, dpi = 300)
ggsave("Violin of scores_subset.png", p8, width = 16, height = 8, dpi = 300)
ggsave("Distribution of cell types_subset.png", p9, width = 16, height = 8, dpi = 300)
ggsave("Violin of cell types_subset.png", p10, width = 16, height = 8, dpi = 300)
```


```{r}
# Get total number of cells
total_cells <- ncol(mTSCLC_subset.query)

# All predictions (including uncertain)
print("All predictions (including low confidence):")
all_predictions <- table(mTSCLC_subset.query$predicted.id)
print(all_predictions)
print(paste("Total cells:", total_cells))
print(paste("Percentage of total cells:"))
print(round(prop.table(all_predictions) * 100, 2))

# High confidence predictions only
print("\nHigh confidence predictions only:")
high_conf_predictions <- table(mTSCLC_subset.query$predicted.id[mTSCLC_subset.query$high_conf_celltype != "Uncertain"])
print(high_conf_predictions)
print(paste("Total high confidence cells:", sum(high_conf_predictions)))
print(paste("Percentage of total cells that are high confidence:"))
print(round(prop.table(high_conf_predictions) * 100, 2))

# Create a comparison data frame
comparison_df <- data.frame(
  Cell_Type = names(all_predictions),
  Total_Predictions = as.numeric(all_predictions),
  High_Confidence = as.numeric(high_conf_predictions[names(all_predictions)]),
  row.names = NULL
)

# Calculate percentages and differences
comparison_df <- comparison_df %>%
  mutate(
    Percent_Total = round(Total_Predictions / total_cells * 100, 2),
    Percent_High_Conf = round(High_Confidence / total_cells * 100, 2),
    Percent_Confident = round(High_Confidence / Total_Predictions * 100, 2)
  )

# Print formatted comparison table
print("\nDetailed comparison:")
print(comparison_df)

# Optional: Create a visualization of the comparison
ggplot(comparison_df, aes(x = Cell_Type)) +
  geom_bar(aes(y = Total_Predictions, fill = "All Predictions"), stat = "identity", position = "dodge", alpha = 0.6) +
  geom_bar(aes(y = High_Confidence, fill = "High Confidence"), stat = "identity", position = "dodge", alpha = 0.8) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("All Predictions" = "grey", "High Confidence" = "darkblue")) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)) +
  labs(title = "Comparison of All vs High Confidence Predictions",
       x = "Cell Type",
       y = "Number of Cells",
       fill = "Prediction Type")

# Save the plot
ggsave("prediction_confidence_comparison_subset.png", width = 10, height = 6)
```
```{r}
# Get total number of cells
total_cells <- 2286	

# Get all unique cell types first to ensure we capture all categories
all_cell_types <- unique(mTSCLC_subset.query$predicted.id)

# Create the counts separately first
total_pred_table <- table(mTSCLC_subset.query$predicted.id)
high_conf_table <- table(mTSCLC_subset.query$predicted.id[mTSCLC_subset.query$high_conf_celltype != "Uncertain"])

# Create a data frame with all cell types and fill in the counts
comparison_df <- data.frame(
  Cell_Type = all_cell_types,
  Total_Predictions = as.numeric(total_pred_table[all_cell_types]),
  High_Confidence = 0  # Initialize with zeros
) 

# Fill in high confidence counts where they exist
high_conf_cell_types <- names(high_conf_table)
comparison_df$High_Confidence[match(high_conf_cell_types, comparison_df$Cell_Type)] <- 
  as.numeric(high_conf_table)

# Calculate percentages
comparison_df <- comparison_df %>%
  mutate(
    Percent_Total = round(Total_Predictions / total_cells * 100, 2),
    Percent_High_Conf = round(High_Confidence / total_cells * 100, 2),
    Percent_Confident = round(High_Confidence / Total_Predictions * 100, 2)
  )

# Create labels for the legend
total_count <- sum(comparison_df$Total_Predictions)
high_conf_count <- sum(comparison_df$High_Confidence)
total_percent <- round(total_count/total_cells * 100, 1)
high_conf_percent <- round(high_conf_count/total_cells * 100, 1)

legend_labels <- c(
  `All Predictions` = paste0("All Predictions\n(", total_count, " cells, ", total_percent, "%)"),
  `High Confidence` = paste0("High Confidence\n(", high_conf_count, " cells, ", high_conf_percent, "%)")
)

# Create the visualization
ggplot(comparison_df, aes(x = Cell_Type)) +
  geom_bar(aes(y = Total_Predictions, fill = "All Predictions"), 
           stat = "identity", 
           position = "dodge", 
           alpha = 0.6) +
  geom_bar(aes(y = High_Confidence, fill = "High Confidence"), 
           stat = "identity", 
           position = "dodge", 
           alpha = 0.8) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c("All Predictions" = "grey", "High Confidence" = "darkblue"),
    labels = legend_labels
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    limits = c(0, total_cells),
    expand = c(0, 0),
    breaks = seq(0, total_cells, by = 2000)
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = 16),  # Increased size from default
    legend.position = "top",
    legend.box.spacing = unit(0.5, "cm"),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 16),
    plot.title = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold"),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 16)
) +
  labs(
    title = "Comparison of All vs High Confidence Predictions",
    subtitle = paste0("Total cells analyzed: ", total_cells),
    x = "Cell Type",
    y = "Number of Cells",
    fill = "Prediction Type"
  )

# Save the plot
ggsave(
  "prediction_confidence_comparison_subset2.png", 
  width = 12, 
  height = 8, 
  dpi = 300
)

# Print summary statistics
print("Summary Statistics:")
print(paste("Total cells analyzed:", total_cells))
print(paste("Total high confidence cells:", high_conf_count))
print(paste("Percent high confidence:", high_conf_percent, "%"))
print("\nDetailed statistics by cell type:")
print(comparison_df)

# Save detailed statistics
write.csv(comparison_df, "cell_type_prediction_statistics_subset.csv", row.names = FALSE)
```

```{r}
library(Seurat)
library(ggplot2)
library(patchwork)
library(ggrepel)
library(dplyr)
library(viridis)

# Define custom colors for cell types using your color scheme
cell_colors <- c(
  "mXEN" = "#D98880",
  "mTSC" = "#F39C12",
  "Serum/LIF mESC" = "#F4D03F",
  "2i/LIF mESC" = "#52BE80",
  "Amniotic Mesoderm" = "#5DADE2",
  "Amniotic Ectoderm" = "#AF7AC5",
  "Uncertain" = "#515151"  # Keep grey for uncertain cells
)

# 1. Reference structure
p1 <- DimPlot(invitro.cells.combined.ref, 
              reduction = "umap",
              group.by = "new_cluster_ids", 
              label = FALSE, 
              repel = TRUE,
              cols = cell_colors) +
  ggtitle("Reference Structure") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    axis.line = element_line(color = "black"),
    plot.title = element_text(size = 20, face = "bold"),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 18, face = "bold"),
    axis.text = element_text(size = 14),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 20),
    legend.key.size = unit(5, "lines"),
    legend.title = element_blank()
  )

# 2. Original structure with all predictions
p2_all <- DimPlot(mTSCLC_subset.query, 
                  reduction = "umap",
                  group.by = "predicted.id", 
                  label = FALSE, 
                  repel = TRUE,
                  cols = cell_colors) +
  ggtitle("All Predictions") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    axis.line = element_line(color = "black"),
    plot.title = element_text(size = 20, face = "bold"),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 18, face = "bold"),
    axis.text = element_text(size = 14),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 20),
    legend.key.size = unit(5, "lines"),
    legend.title = element_blank()
  )

# 3. High confidence predictions with layered plotting
umap_coords <- Embeddings(mTSCLC_subset.query, "umap")
plot_data <- data.frame(
  UMAP_1 = umap_coords[,1],
  UMAP_2 = umap_coords[,2],
  celltype = mTSCLC_subset.query$high_conf_celltype
)

p2_high_conf <- ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = UMAP_1, y = UMAP_2, color = celltype)) +
  # Plot uncertain cells first
  geom_point(data = subset(plot_data, celltype == "Uncertain"),
             color = "#515151", alpha = 0.2, size = 0.5) +
  # Plot high confidence cells on top
  geom_point(data = subset(plot_data, celltype != "Uncertain"),
             size = 1,
             alpha = 0.6) +
  scale_color_manual(values = cell_colors) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    axis.line = element_line(color = "black"),
    plot.title = element_text(size = 20, face = "bold"),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 18, face = "bold"),
    axis.text = element_text(size = 14),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 20),
    legend.key.size = unit(5, "lines"),
    legend.title = element_blank()
  ) + 
  guides(color = guide_legend(override.aes = list(size = 6, alpha = 1))) +
  ggtitle("High Confidence Cell Type Predictions") + 
  coord_fixed()

# Create a combined plot
combined_plot <- p1 | p2_all | p2_high_conf

# Save plots
ggsave("1_reference_structure_subset.png", p1, width = 10, height = 8, dpi = 300)
ggsave("2_all_predictions_subset.png", p2_all, width = 10, height = 8, dpi = 300)
ggsave("3_high_confidence_predictions_subset.png", p2_high_conf, width = 10, height = 8, dpi = 300)
ggsave("combined_umap_visualization_subset.png", combined_plot, width = 24, height = 8, dpi = 300)

# Print cell type statistics
print("All predictions (including uncertain):")
print(table(mTSCLC_subset.query$predicted.id))

print("\nHigh confidence predictions only:")
high_conf_counts <- table(mTSCLC_subset.query$high_conf_celltype[mTSCLC_subset.query$high_conf_celltype != "Uncertain"])
print(high_conf_counts)

# Calculate and print percentages
total_cells <- sum(table(mTSCLC_subset.query$high_conf_celltype))
percentages <- (table(mTSCLC_subset.query$high_conf_celltype) / total_cells) * 100

print("\nPercentages of each cell type:")
print(round(percentages, 2))

# Create a summary data frame
summary_df <- data.frame(
  CellType = names(high_conf_counts),
  Count = as.numeric(high_conf_counts),
  Percentage = round(as.numeric(high_conf_counts) / sum(high_conf_counts) * 100, 2)
)

# Save summary statistics
write.csv(summary_df, "cell_type_summary_subset.csv", row.names = FALSE)
```
```{r}
cluster_color_palette <- c(
  "mXEN" = "#D98880",
  "mTSC" = "#F39C12",
  "Serum/LIF mESC" = "#F4D03F",
  "2i/LIF mESC" = "#52BE80",
  "Amniotic Mesoderm" = "#5DADE2",
  "Amniotic Ectoderm" = "#AF7AC5",
  "Uncertain" = "#515151"  # Keep grey for uncertain cells
)
# Stacked bar chart of cell type proportions
ggplot(data.frame(celltype = mTSCLC_subset.query$high_conf_celltype), aes(x = "All", fill = celltype)) +
  geom_bar(position = "fill") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = cluster_color_palette) +
  theme_minimal() +
  ggtitle("Proportion of Predicted Cell Types")

# Prediction score distributions by cell type
ggplot(mTSCLC_subset.query@meta.data, aes(x = predicted.id, y = prediction.score.max)) +
  geom_violin(aes(fill = predicted.id)) +
  geom_boxplot(width = 0.1) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = cluster_color_palette) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))
```
```{r}
library(Seurat)
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(gridExtra)
library(ggtext)

# Define extended color palette for clusters
color_palette <- c(
  "#FFD700",  # Gold (Topaz-like)
  "#FF8C00",  # Dark Orange (Citrine-like)
  "#FF4500",  # Orange Red (Fire Opal-like)
  "#DC143C",  # Crimson (Ruby-like)
  "#8B0000",  # Dark Red 
  "#800080",  # Purple (Alexandrite-like)
  "#4B0082",  # Indigo (Sapphire-like)
  "#9400D3",  # Dark Violet (Amethyst-like)
  "#6A5ACD",  # Slate Blue (Tanzanite-like)
  "#4682B4",  # Steel Blue (Aquamarine-like)
  "#00BFFF",  # Deep Sky Blue (Blue Topaz-like)
  "#008080",  # Teal (Emerald-like)
  "#20B2AA",  # Light Sea Green (Turquoise-like)
  "#32CD32",  # Lime Green (Peridot-like)
  "#00FF00"   # Bright Lime
)

# Filter for high confidence predictions
high_conf_data <- mTSCLC_subset.query@meta.data %>%
  filter(high_conf_celltype != "Uncertain") %>%
  mutate(cluster = as.character(SCT_snn_res.0.6))

# Calculate number of cells per cluster and predicted cell type
cluster_celltype_counts <- table(high_conf_data$cluster,
                               high_conf_data$high_conf_celltype)

# Calculate percentages
celltype_cluster_percentages <- prop.table(cluster_celltype_counts, margin = 2) * 100

# Convert to data frame
df <- as.data.frame(celltype_cluster_percentages)
colnames(df) <- c("Cluster", "CellType", "Percentage")

# Add count information
df$Count <- as.vector(cluster_celltype_counts)

# Calculate total cells per cell type
total_cells_per_celltype <- colSums(cluster_celltype_counts)

# Add total cells per cell type to the dataframe
df$TotalInCellType <- total_cells_per_celltype[df$CellType]

# Function to create a single pie chart
create_pie_chart <- function(cell_type) {
  celltype_data <- df[df$CellType == cell_type,]
  
  # Create custom labels with counts and percentages
  custom_labels <- paste0(
    "Cluster ", celltype_data$Cluster, 
    "\n(", celltype_data$Count, "/", celltype_data$TotalInCellType, ")",
    "\n", sprintf("%.1f%%", celltype_data$Percentage)
  )
  
  total_cells <- sum(celltype_data$Count)
  
  p <- ggplot(celltype_data, aes(x = "", y = Percentage, fill = Cluster)) +
    geom_bar(stat = "identity", width = 1) +
    coord_polar("y", start = 0) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = color_palette[1:length(unique(df$Cluster))],
                     labels = custom_labels) +
    theme_void() +
    theme(
      legend.position = "right",
      legend.title = element_text(size = 16),
      legend.text = element_text(size = 12),
      legend.key.size = unit(2, "cm"),
      plot.title = element_text(size = 20, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
      plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 16, hjust = 0.5)
    ) +
    ggtitle(cell_type) +
    labs(subtitle = paste("Total high confidence cells:", total_cells)) +
    guides(fill = guide_legend(title = "Cluster\n(Count/Total)\nPercentage",
                             override.aes = list(size = 1)))
  
  return(p)
}

# Create pie charts for each cell type
pie_charts <- lapply(sort(unique(df$CellType)), create_pie_chart)

# Arrange pie charts in a grid
grid_arranged <- grid.arrange(grobs = pie_charts, ncol = 3)

# Save the grid of pie charts
ggsave("high_confidence_celltype_by_cluster_pie_charts_Subset.png",
       grid_arranged,
       width = 30,
       height = 20,
       units = "in",
       dpi = 300)

# Save data to CSV
write.csv(df, "high_confidence_celltype_by_cluster_percentages_and_counts_subset.csv",
          row.names = FALSE)

# Print summaries
print("Summary of high confidence predictions by cluster:")
print(table(high_conf_data$cluster, high_conf_data$high_conf_celltype))

print("\nCluster contribution to each cell type (%):")
print(celltype_cluster_percentages)

print("\nTotal cells per cell type:")
print(total_cells_per_celltype)

# Additional analysis: Cluster composition
print("\nPercentage of each cluster that is high confidence:")
cluster_totals <- table(mTSCLC_subset.query$SCT_snn_res.0.6)
high_conf_by_cluster <- table(high_conf_data$cluster)
cluster_percentages <- (high_conf_by_cluster / cluster_totals) * 100
print(cluster_percentages)
```

```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(pheatmap)
library(RColorBrewer)

cluster_color_palette <- c(
  "#FFD700",  # Gold (Topaz-like)
  "#FF8C00",  # Dark Orange (Citrine-like)
  "#FF4500",  # Orange Red (Fire Opal-like)
  "#DC143C",  # Crimson (Ruby-like)
  "#8B0000",  # Dark Red 
  "#800080",  # Purple (Alexandrite-like)
  "#4B0082",  # Indigo (Sapphire-like)
  "#9400D3",  # Dark Violet (Amethyst-like)
  "#6A5ACD",  # Slate Blue (Tanzanite-like)
  "#4682B4",  # Steel Blue (Aquamarine-like)
  "#00BFFF",  # Deep Sky Blue (Blue Topaz-like)
  "#008080",  # Teal (Emerald-like)
  "#20B2AA",  # Light Sea Green (Turquoise-like)
  "#32CD32",  # Lime Green (Peridot-like)
  "#00FF00"   # Bright Lime
)

# Reshape the data for the heatmap
heatmap_data <- final_counts %>%
  select(Cluster, predicted.celltype, percentage_after) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = predicted.celltype, values_from = percentage_after, values_fill = list(percentage_after = 0))

# Add Amniotic Mesoderm column with 0%
heatmap_data$`Amniotic Mesoderm` <- 0

# Ensure all cell types of interest are present
for (cell_type in cell_types_of_interest) {
  if (!(cell_type %in% colnames(heatmap_data))) {
    heatmap_data[[cell_type]] <- 0
  }
}

# Convert to matrix while preserving row names
heatmap_matrix <- as.matrix(heatmap_data %>% column_to_rownames("Cluster"))

# Reorder columns to match cell_types_of_interest
heatmap_matrix <- heatmap_matrix[, cell_types_of_interest]

print("Heatmap Matrix dimensions:")
print(dim(heatmap_matrix))
print("Heatmap Matrix row names:")
print(rownames(heatmap_matrix))
print("Heatmap Matrix column names:")
print(colnames(heatmap_matrix))

# Create annotations
col_annotation <- data.frame(CellType = factor(colnames(heatmap_matrix), levels = cell_types_of_interest))
rownames(col_annotation) <- colnames(heatmap_matrix)

row_annotation <- data.frame(Cluster = factor(rownames(heatmap_matrix)))
rownames(row_annotation) <- rownames(heatmap_matrix)

print("Column Annotation:")
print(col_annotation)
print("Row Annotation:")
print(row_annotation)

# Define color palettes
celltype_colors <- setNames(
  colorRampPalette(cell_type_colors)(length(cell_types_of_interest)),
  cell_types_of_interest
)

print("Cell Type Colors:")
print(celltype_colors)

cluster_colors <- setNames(
  cluster_color_palette[1:nrow(heatmap_matrix)],
  rownames(heatmap_matrix)
)

print("Cluster Colors:")
print(cluster_colors)

# Define annotation colors
annotation_colors <- list(
  CellType = celltype_colors,
  Cluster = cluster_colors
)

print("Annotation Colors:")
print(str(annotation_colors))

# Create the heatmap
png("mTSCLC_celltype_percentage_heatmap_fixed.png", width = 6000, height = 5000, res = 300)
pheatmap(heatmap_matrix,
         display_numbers = TRUE,
         number_format = "%.2f%%",
         number_color = "white",
         cluster_rows = TRUE,
         cluster_cols = FALSE,
         color = colorRampPalette(c("#000080", "#4682B4", "#800020"))(100),
         main = "In Vitro Cell Types Identified in mTSCLC clusters",
         fontsize = 20,
         fontsize_number = 20,
         cellwidth = 100,
         cellheight = 60,
         angle_col="45",
         annotation_col = col_annotation,
         annotation_row = row_annotation,
         annotation_colors = annotation_colors,
                  annotation_names_row = FALSE,
         annotation_names_col = FALSE,
         border_color = "darkgrey",
          show_rownames = TRUE,
                  treeheight_row = 400,
         treeheight_col = 0,
         fontsize_row = 18,
         fontsize_col = 18)
dev.off()

print("Heatmap creation attempt completed.")
```
```{r}
# Density plots of prediction scores
ggplot(mTSCLC_subset.query@meta.data, aes(x = prediction.score.max)) +
  geom_density(aes(fill = predicted.id), alpha = 0.5) +
  facet_wrap(~predicted.id) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = cluster_color_palette)

# Spatial distribution of prediction confidence
FeaturePlot(mTSCLC_subset.query, 
           features = "prediction.score.max",
           reduction = "umap")
```

```{r}
# Find DEGs between Serum/LIF mESC and mTSC from reference dataset
Idents(invitro.cells.combined.ref) <- "new_cluster_ids"

# Get Serum/LIF mESC markers (comparing to mTSC)
mesc_markers <- FindMarkers(invitro.cells.combined.ref, 
                          ident.1 = "Serum/LIF mESC", 
                          ident.2 = "mTSC", 
                          min.pct = 0.25)

# Get mTSC markers (comparing to Serum/LIF mESC)
mtsc_markers <- FindMarkers(invitro.cells.combined.ref, 
                         ident.1 = "mTSC", 
                         ident.2 = "Serum/LIF mESC", 
                         min.pct = 0.25)

# Get top 100 genes for each cell type
mesc_genes <- rownames(mesc_markers %>% 
                    filter(avg_log2FC > 0) %>% 
                    arrange(desc(avg_log2FC)) %>% 
                    head(100))

mtsc_genes <- rownames(mtsc_markers %>% 
                    filter(avg_log2FC > 0) %>% 
                    arrange(desc(avg_log2FC)) %>% 
                    head(100))

# Print top genes for verification
print("Top 10 Serum/LIF mESC markers:")
print(head(mesc_genes, 10))
print("\nTop 10 mTSC markers:")
print(head(mtsc_genes, 10))

# Save gene lists
write.csv(data.frame(gene = mesc_genes), "top_100_mesc_genes_subset.csv")
write.csv(data.frame(gene = mtsc_genes), "top_100_mtsc_genes_subset.csv")
# First calculate module scores on the full dataset
# First ensure the genes exist in the full dataset
mesc_genes_present <- intersect(mesc_genes, rownames(cells.combined.p12))
```
