```{r}
mtsc_genes_present <- intersect(mtsc_genes, rownames(cells.combined.p12))

print(paste("Number of Serum/LIF mESC genes found in dataset:", length(mesc_genes_present)))
print(paste("Number of mTSC genes found in dataset:", length(mtsc_genes_present)))

# Calculate scores on the full dataset
cells.combined.p12 <- AddModuleScore(
    object = cells.combined.p12,
    features = list(mesc_genes_present),
    name = "mESC_score")

cells.combined.p12 <- AddModuleScore(
    object = cells.combined.p12,
    features = list(mtsc_genes_present),
    name = "mTSC_score")

# Now subset using your original code
clusters_of_interest <- c("2", "4", "6", "7")
subset_p12 <- subset(x = cells.combined.p12, 
                    subset = SCT_snn_res.0.6 %in% clusters_of_interest)  

# Verify the subsetting worked
table(Idents(subset_p12))

# Normalize and scale the data
subset_p12 <- NormalizeData(subset_p12)
subset_p12 <- ScaleData(subset_p12)

# Run PCA
subset_p12 <- RunPCA(subset_p12)

# Run UMAP on the subset
subset_p12 <- RunUMAP(subset_p12, dims = 1:20)

# Create visualization of the subset using your previous color scheme
color_palette_subset <- c(
  "2" = "#DC143C",  # Cluster 2 - Crimson
  "4" = "#4B0082",  # Cluster 4 - Indigo
  "6" = "#4682B4",  # Cluster 6 - Steel Blue
  "7" = "#008080"   # Cluster 7 - Teal
)

# First get the maximum absolute value for symmetric scaling
max_abs_value <- max(abs(c(
    subset_p12$mESC_score1,
    subset_p12$mTSC_score1)))

# Create plots with symmetric scales and custom colors
p1 <- FeaturePlot(subset_p12,
                  features = "mESC_score1",
                  reduction = "umap",
                  order = TRUE) +
    scale_color_gradientn(
        colors = c("navy", "lightgrey", "red"),
        limits = c(-max_abs_value, max_abs_value)
    ) +
    theme_minimal() +
    ggtitle("Serum/LIF mESC Score") +
    theme(
        plot.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold"),
        axis.title = element_text(size = 12),
        legend.title = element_text(size = 10),
        legend.text = element_text(size = 8)
    ) +
    coord_fixed()

p2 <- FeaturePlot(subset_p12,
                  features = "mTSC_score1",
                  reduction = "umap",
                  order = TRUE) +
    scale_color_gradientn(
        colors = c("navy", "lightgrey", "red"),
        limits = c(-max_abs_value, max_abs_value)
    ) +
    theme_minimal() +
    ggtitle("mTSC Score") +
    theme(
        plot.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold"),
        axis.title = element_text(size = 12),
        legend.title = element_text(size = 10),
        legend.text = element_text(size = 8)
    ) +
    coord_fixed()

# Combine plots
score_plots <- p1 | p2

# Save the plots
ggsave("cell_type_scores_subset_preserved.png", score_plots, width = 15, height = 7, dpi = 300)

# Create categorical plots based on scores
score_cutoff <- 0  # You might want to adjust this based on the score distribution
subset_p12$mESC_category <- ifelse(subset_p12$mESC_score1 > score_cutoff, "High mESC", "Low mESC")
subset_p12$mTSC_category <- ifelse(subset_p12$mTSC_score1 > score_cutoff, "High mTSC", "Low mTSC")

# Create categorical UMAPs with custom colors
categorical_umaps <- DimPlot(subset_p12,
                           reduction = "umap",
                           group.by = c("mESC_category", "mTSC_category"),
                           ncol = 2,
                           cols = c(
                               "High mESC" = "#F4D03F",
                               "Low mESC" = "grey80",
                               "High mTSC" = "#F39C12",
                               "Low mTSC" = "grey80"
                           )) +
    theme(
        plot.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold"),
        axis.title = element_text(size = 12),
        legend.title = element_text(size = 10),
        legend.text = element_text(size = 8)
    )

ggsave("categorical_scores_subset_preserved.png", categorical_umaps, width = 15, height = 7, dpi = 300)

# Print score statistics
print("\nScore statistics:")
print("Serum/LIF mESC scores:")
print(summary(subset_p12$mESC_score1))
print("\nmTSC scores:")
print(summary(subset_p12$mTSC_score1))

# Calculate correlation between scores
score_correlation <- cor(subset_p12$mESC_score1, subset_p12$mTSC_score1)
print("\nCorrelation between mESC and mTSC scores:")
print(score_correlation)

# Save the new Seurat object
saveRDS(subset_p12, "subset_p12_clusters_2467_preserved_scores.rds")
```


```{r}
# Ensure the query object has expression data available
if (ncol(mTSCLC_subset.query) == 0) {
  stop("No expression data available in mTSCLC_subset.query.")
}

# Check for the presence of gene names in the query data
gene_of_interest <- "Nanog"  # Replace with the gene you're interested in

# Check if the gene of interest is present in the query dataset
if (!gene_of_interest %in% rownames(mTSCLC_subset.query)) {
  stop(paste(gene_of_interest, "is not found in the mTSCLC_subset.query dataset."))
}

# Filter the high-confidence cells
mapped_cells <- mTSCLC_subset.query[, mTSCLC_subset.query$high_conf_celltype != "Uncertain"]

# Create a feature plot for the gene of interest
feature_plot <- FeaturePlot(mapped_cells, features = gene_of_interest, 
                             reduction = "ref.umap") + 
  ggtitle(paste("Feature Plot for", gene_of_interest, "in Mapped Cells")) +
  theme_minimal()

# Save the plot
ggsave(paste0("Feature_Plot_", gene_of_interest, "_Mapped_Cells.png"), 
       feature_plot, width = 12, height = 10, dpi = 300)

# Optionally, display the plot in R
print(feature_plot)

```
```{r}
# Load necessary libraries
library(Seurat)
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)

# Specify the gene of interest (Nanog)
gene_of_interest <- "Nanog"

# Check if the gene of interest is present in the query dataset
if (!gene_of_interest %in% rownames(mTSCLC_subset.query)) {
  stop(paste(gene_of_interest, "is not found in the mTSCLC_subset.query dataset."))
}

# Get UMAP coordinates for both datasets
ref_umap <- Embeddings(invitro.cells.combined.clean, reduction = "umap")
query_umap <- Embeddings(mTSCLC_subset.query, reduction = "ref.umap") # or use "umap" if ref.umap doesn't exist

# Fetch expression data for Nanog for both datasets
ref_data <- FetchData(invitro.cells.combined.clean, vars = c(gene_of_interest))
query_data <- FetchData(mTSCLC_subset.query, vars = c(gene_of_interest))

# Combine the coordinates and expression data
combined_data <- rbind(
  data.frame(umap_1 = ref_umap[, 1], umap_2 = ref_umap[, 2], 
             expression = ref_data[[gene_of_interest]], source = "Reference", 
             high_conf_celltype = invitro.cells.combined.clean$condition),
  data.frame(umap_1 = query_umap[, 1], umap_2 = query_umap[, 2], 
             expression = query_data[[gene_of_interest]], source = "Query", 
             high_conf_celltype = mTSCLC_subset.query$high_conf_celltype)
)

# Remove Uncertain category from Query
combined_data <- combined_data %>%
  filter(!(source == "Query" & high_conf_celltype == "Uncertain"))

# Create the feature plot
feature_plot <- ggplot(combined_data, aes(x = umap_1, y = umap_2)) +
  geom_point(data = subset(combined_data, source == "Reference"), 
             color = "grey", alpha = 0.3, size = 1) +  # Reference data in grey
  geom_point(data = subset(combined_data, source == "Query"), 
             aes(color = expression), size = 1.5) +  # Query data colored by expression
  scale_color_gradient(low = "blue", high = "red") +  # Blue to red color gradient
  theme_minimal() +
  ggtitle(paste("Nanog Expression in Mapped Cells")) +
  labs(color = "Nanog Expression") +  # Color legend title
  theme(
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    axis.line = element_line(color = "black"),
    plot.title = element_text(size = 20, face = "bold"),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 14),
    axis.text = element_text(size = 12),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 12),
    legend.key.size = unit(1.5, "lines")
  )

# Save the plot
ggsave("Nanog_Expression_Mapped_Cells_with_Reference.png", 
       feature_plot, width = 12, height = 10, dpi = 300)

# Optionally, display the plot in R
print(feature_plot)

```


```{r}
# Load necessary libraries
library(Seurat)
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)

# Specify the genes of interest
genes_of_interest <- c("Nanog", "Pou5f1", "Sox2", "Gata3","Cdx2","Elf5")

# Initialize an empty list to store plots
plots <- list()

# Loop through each gene to create individual plots
for (gene_of_interest in genes_of_interest) {
  
  # Check if the gene of interest is present in the query dataset
  if (!gene_of_interest %in% rownames(mTSCLC_subset.query)) {
    stop(paste(gene_of_interest, "is not found in the mTSCLC_subset.query dataset."))
  }

  # Get UMAP coordinates for both datasets
  ref_umap <- Embeddings(invitro.cells.combined.clean, reduction = "umap")
  query_umap <- Embeddings(mTSCLC_subset.query, reduction = "ref.umap") # or use "umap" if ref.umap doesn't exist

  # Fetch expression data for the gene of interest for both datasets
  ref_data <- FetchData(invitro.cells.combined.clean, vars = c(gene_of_interest))
  query_data <- FetchData(mTSCLC_subset.query, vars = c(gene_of_interest))

  # Combine the coordinates and expression data
  combined_data <- rbind(
    data.frame(umap_1 = ref_umap[, 1], umap_2 = ref_umap[, 2], 
               expression = ref_data[[gene_of_interest]], source = "Reference", 
               high_conf_celltype = invitro.cells.combined.clean$condition),
    data.frame(umap_1 = query_umap[, 1], umap_2 = query_umap[, 2], 
               expression = query_data[[gene_of_interest]], source = "Query", 
               high_conf_celltype = mTSCLC_subset.query$high_conf_celltype)
  )

  # Remove Uncertain category from Query
  combined_data <- combined_data %>%
    filter(!(source == "Query" & high_conf_celltype == "Uncertain"))

  # Create the feature plot
  feature_plot <- ggplot(combined_data, aes(x = umap_1, y = umap_2)) +
    geom_point(data = subset(combined_data, source == "Reference"), 
               color = "grey", alpha = 0.3, size = 1) +  # Reference data in grey
    geom_point(data = subset(combined_data, source == "Query"), 
               aes(color = expression), size = 1.5) +  # Query data colored by expression
    scale_color_gradient(low = "blue", high = "red") +  # Blue to red color gradient
    theme_minimal() +
    ggtitle(paste(gene_of_interest, "Expression in Mapped Cells")) +
    labs(color = paste(gene_of_interest, "Expression")) +  # Color legend title
    theme(
      panel.grid = element_blank(),
      axis.line = element_line(color = "black"),
      plot.title = element_text(size = 20, face = "bold"),
      axis.title = element_text(size = 14),
      axis.text = element_text(size = 12),
      legend.text = element_text(size = 12),
      legend.key.size = unit(1.5, "lines")
    )

  # Save the plot
  plot_filename <- paste0(gene_of_interest, "_Expression_Mapped_Cells_with_Reference.png")
  ggsave(plot_filename, feature_plot, width = 12, height = 10, dpi = 300)

  # Optionally, store the plot in the list for displaying later
  plots[[gene_of_interest]] <- feature_plot
}

# Optionally, display all plots
for (gene in genes_of_interest) {
  print(plots[[gene]])
}
```
